# papers Documentation

> Static documentation template

A minimal docs starter with install-ready defaults, generated content, and static-host deployment support.

## Getting Started

- [Introduction](/docs/getting-started/introduction): `papers` is a static documentation template for teams that want a polished docs site without standing up a backend or CMS first.
- [Quick Start](/docs/getting-started/quick-start): Use this path if you want to fork the template, replace the sample content, and get your own docs site running as quickly as possible.
- [Installation](/docs/getting-started/installation): - Node.js 18 or newer

## User Guide

- [Basic Usage](/docs/user-guide/basic-usage): Once the site is running, the normal workflow is simple: edit Markdown, preview locally, and ship a static build.
- [Advanced Features](/docs/user-guide/advanced-features): This template ships with more than a basic Markdown renderer.
- [Configuration](/docs/user-guide/configuration): Most template changes happen in a small set of files.
- [Troubleshooting](/docs/user-guide/troubleshooting): Your host is probably missing an SPA rewrite.
- [Command Palette](/docs/user-guide/command-palette): The command palette is the fastest way to move through the site.
- [Interactive Map](/docs/user-guide/interactive-map): The interactive map renders the documentation tree as a graph in the right sidebar.

## API Reference

- [Overview](/docs/api-reference/overview): This section documents the internal APIs and generated artifacts that power the template.
- [Content Pipeline](/docs/api-reference/content-pipeline): The content pipeline converts Markdown source files into static artifacts the app can ship directly.
- [Runtime APIs](/docs/api-reference/runtime-apis): Loads a single document from the generated docs content store.

## Developer Guides

- [Code Examples](/docs/developer-guides/code-examples): This section shows the supported authoring patterns in the current Vite-based template.
- [Best Practices](/docs/developer-guides/best-practices): Treat `shared/documentation-config.js` as the source of truth for navigation and page tags.
- [Design System](/docs/developer-guides/design-system): A comprehensive guide to the design system, color palettes, typography, and components that make up the documentation platform.
- [UI Configuration](/docs/developer-guides/ui-configuration): The template keeps content in the docs framework and exposes a few layout controls through code.
- [Icon Customization](/docs/developer-guides/icon-customization): This documentation site supports custom icons throughout the interface. You can easily add new icons from [Iconify Design](https://iconify.design) or create your own custom SVG icons.

## Platform Guides

- [Cloudflare](/docs/deployment/platforms/cloudflare): Use these settings in Cloudflare Pages:
- [Vercel](/docs/deployment/platforms/vercel): Use these values when importing the repo:
- [Netlify](/docs/deployment/platforms/netlify): Use these settings in Netlify:

## Deployment

- [Overview](/docs/deployment/overview): This project is a static site build with SPA routing.
- [Production Setup](/docs/deployment/production-setup): Before you ship, set the site metadata you actually want users to see.
